...Then maybe... Mr. Dylan would like to see me in a swimsuit?
Definitely not!
Oh, sorry. I am only a robot, after all.
Err, I'm the one who's sorry. I didn't mean it like that.
Don't worry about it. By the way, have you seen the sea, Mr. Dylan?
Um, I have. I'm thinking it was that time we loaned Master SilverAsh an aircraft last summer after a schedule change mixed things up. I flew him there.
It turns out, the place was a major vacation spot. Master SilverAsh made me a VIP guest.
Man, that's when I realized how us poor folks could scarcely imagine what the rich live like.
Here, I've got the pictures with me.
You long for this kind of life, Mr. Dylan?
Hm? I mean... that was a really great couple of days, but if my whole life was like that, I don't think I'd ever get used to it.
I'm more of an instant-noodles-in-the-cockpit kind of guy. With an inverted sleep schedule to boot.
Oh you can't do that. Let me formulate a healthy lifestyle plan for you.
No thanks, I'm good!
Too bad. The lovely Ms. Closure created this feature for me, but no one uses it. Not her, not the Doctor.
Haha, sounds about right.
Oh, but that reminds me, the tribes here live so much better than I thought. They really want for nothing.
Yes, life here is less convenient and comfortable than the world we know, but not to any significant degree.
According to my records, your health has improved in the past two days.
Really?
Really.
Turns out returning to nature will do that for you. Heh. I guess this is the life that everyone came from.
Oh yeah, I just remembered, Gavial said something about a gigantic waterfall in the rainforest that way. I'd love to go there when the Doctor gets back.
They may have already gone that way.
The Doctor is a good boss. And I know our friends wouldn't abandon us to go play under the waterfall!
True. Then let's wait patiently.
Is there a 'Dylan' here?
That's me!
Huh? You speak Sargonian?
Yes. And you're here with the machine. Right, it must be you.
The one called 'Doctor' asked me to give you this note.
Hm? Let's see here...
...Lancet-2, looks like we've got work to do.
Wow! I could hear the roar of the water from so far away, I never knew waterfalls even got this big!
Hah, I didn't steer you wrong, did I?
Sure enough. I guess I'll forgive you~
Haha! It really is an incredible waterfall.
Looks like Tomimi isn't here yet, so let's take a little break.
Hooray! I can finally get some use out of my swimsuit~
You got a lotta fight in you, squirt.
Kemar, well aware of the language barrier, communicates with her stance, rather than words.
Heh. Seeing as I've got nothing else to do, we might as well go a few rounds.
Blaze is really going at it hard with that girl over there.
Sure is.
I don't see what's so fun about fighting.
...I heard you singin' a whole 'nother tune last time you was fightin', Utage.
Ahahaha. I guess you're right.
Hey, Croissant, that isn't a swimsuit, is it?
Ahahaha, ya got me. It's just some outdoorsy getup I snagged. Plenty waterproof, so we'n pretend it's a swimsuit.
You're a real function over form kinda girl, huh?
But hey, it may not be a beach, but playing around a waterfall is pretty great too! It's got that real 'back to nature' vibe. Too bad I didn't bring my camera.
Ya got that right. I ain't never been out in the wilds like this before. I'm havin' a ball!
Hey...
Whassat?
Am I seeing things?
Huh?
Isn't that Kay?
How do you like the waterfall, Doctor?
Hah, I used to love coming here to bathe.
Looks like you've got a lot on your mind, Doctor.
You're cruisin' for a bruisin', Doctor.
From here it's just a short walk to Zumama's tribe.
When I got that letter from Tomimi, I never thought things would turn out like this.
...Oh, Doctor, there's one thing that's been really gnawing at my brain these past few days. We're friends, right? I wanna ask you about it.
Do you think...?
Doooctooor!
What's happening? I'm trying to talk to the Doctor here.
Look over there!
Is that... Kay?
I found it! The Mushroom Sea!
Bath time!
Looks like she's still off in la la land.
But she's just so cute rolling around in the water like that.
Gah, stupid kid.
How about we just let her play for a while...?
Anyway, Doctor, what I wanted to ask was—
Do you think my tail's gotten fatter?
What? Really? I guess I'm going on a diet when we get back.
Come on, Doctor, don't look at me like that. An Archosauria's tail is her most prized asset! This is my life!
Pfft, I knew I shouldn't have asked you.
You don't know, Tomimi's tail wasn't always that fat. And I don't want mine to end up like hers.

opts: What do you think about Zumama?

lines.x: Huh? What do I think about her?
I guess I think she's real tough, and she can make a helluva machine.
I had a feeling you'd be down with tails!
Gavial!
There's Tomimi. You done with that thing?
Yes!
Hmmmmm?
Wh-What?
You're a cutie! Gavial, I never knew you had a friend like this. I thought you just hung around with other muscleheaded lunks like you.
That was uncalled for!
She's got perfect skin, refined features, and a gothy outfit that really goes with her complexion.
Gothy? Is that in?
Well, no. But it's popular with a certain crowd.
I-I picked it up from a magazine. They said it's how they dress in the outside world.
Oh, no, girl, honey. You've got a critically flawed understanding of fashion here. But that's okay. I mean, look at this place. I totally get why you'd come up with something like that.
But don't worry, you just let Big Sister Utage fill you with her bottomless, extra-thick fashion wisdom!
Gavial, I'm borrowing her!
Wha? Whaaaaaaa?
Sure, knock yourself out.
Anyway, we're all here.
I'll see if I can get Kay to sober up...
Break time's over! Kay is back in the fight!
Gavial, she's makin' a break for it again!
What?
Grr, everybody, get your clothes on! We're going after her!
That direction... it's the way to Zumama's tribe...
Hold on! Did you really change that fast?!
Wait up!
Why bother changing? Just go!
Wahhhhhhh!!!
Tch, another failure. He's flying in a nice arc this time though.
Where did he land?
No idea. He'll come running back after a while anyway. Let's keep going.
Okay, what does this tube connect to——
Haha, this looks like a good port, I'm plugging it in!
Hey, is it working?
No. Do you even know what you're doing?!
What are you yelling for? I've never seen an engine like this before, just let me experiment a bit!
I want to try!
I think this tube goes here. What does this button do then? Who cares, just press it.
You're the one who's messing around! It obviously goes here!
What are you two idiots doing? The gas pipe isn't even connected. Let me do it!
Hmm, what does the gas pipe go to... Never mind, this port looks bigger, this is probably it!
Hey, somebody try to start it!
I'm back!
Youngsters nowadays really have no respect for their elders. No one bothered to look for me after I got blasted away!
But, High Priest, you always come back safe. Everyone's gotten used to it!
Stop wasting time, let's get on with the testing!
Yeah, yeah, alright.
Big Ugly, my child, it's time to wake up!
Ohh! Great Chief, it's moving!
This wonderful engine sound! I've never heard anything so amazing!
Haha, told you I'd get it done!
How did you do it?
Just as you saw, Great Chief, it moved just like that!
We plugged this tube here and that one there!
Just like how we built the Big Ugly!
Good.
Ahh, the Big Ugly, once but a pile of scrap metal, is now truly majestic. I'm so moved. It makes me want to sing!
But you're bad at singing.
I'm sorry?! Fine. I think I have a real talent for singing though... Terrible!
Oh? Maybe it's a style problem.
Yes, that's right. That has to be the reason. Next time, I'll try singing in bel canto!
Great Chief, someone broke in!
Is it Gavial? I...
No! It's a weirdo carrying a pile of weapons!
What?
Zumama's tribe should be right ahead.
Damn, look what she's done to her own tribe.
It looks pretty cool!
Eh? It seems pretty lively inside.
Huh? Why are they fighting?
Damn Reunion, you're trying to steal Kay's name now! That's something even more precious than honey biscuits! You'll pay for this!
Gaaaaaaaah!
Where did this weirdo come from? She's too strong!
I can't understand what she's saying. What the hell's a Reunion?!
Eh, isn't that Kay?
Yes.
Sounds like Kay's making a ruckus in there.
Let's calm her down first!
I can't believe Reunion here is so tough. Hmph, I won't lose to you!
Tch, this silly girl.
Wake up!
Hm? Where am I?
Oh, it's Gavial! Everyone else too!
Ehehe. Good morning, Doctor.
Um, Doctor looks a little scary right now. Did I do something wrong?
Doctor, please don't be mad...
Hnng... Doctor, that hurt.
Why did you bop me on the head...?
Looks like she's back to her senses, at least.
Kay, do you remember how you got here?
Umm... I forgot!
Alright.
Come lie down, I'll check and see if there's anything wrong with you. Don't run off again.
Okay~ Ehh, where are we?
We're... Hm, it's a long story. Just lie down already!
Ooh—
Gavial, what the hell are you doing?
Ugh, that's a long story too. Just let me work.
Ten minutes later...
Alright, everything looks okay. Go wait with the Doctor and don't wander off. If you do it again, it's no more honey biscuits for you.
Okay! Honey biscuits!
Are you done?
I'm done.
So now can you tell me what the hell you're doing, Gavial?
*Sigh*, I said it's a long story. Short version is I'm here to look for you.
You're not getting the engine back.
That too. But there's another reason I'm looking for you.
Another reason?
Do you have your people mining ore?
Yes.
...Tch.
Gavial, I never took you for the sore loser type.
Are you that broken up about being beaten by the Big, Ugly Thing?
No, your Big Ugly Whatever is great. I'm totally fine with that.
But if you're putting your people at risk of Oripathy infection for the sake of some ore, then as a doctor I have no choice but to stop you.
Huh? Oripathy? Oh, you're talking about Stone Disease.
I'm not putting anyone at risk.
Huh?
I told them many times to stay away from the depths of the mine.
But your people are getting infected!
There's always someone who doesn't listen. I can't control them all. Isn't that right, Yogi?
Yes, my brother wanted to dig out more ore for the Great Chief, so he went deeper into the mine.
Huh? Why didn't you say so earlier?!
You didn't ask.
That's pretty important...!

opts: You told me they don't take Oripathy seriously here.

lines.x: Agh, fine.
Doctor, did I really not ask?!
Agh, Doctor, take my side here!
Also...
Hey, why are you taking your clothes off?
Huh? Your body...
I've been running around the mine ever since I was a kid. It's only natural I would get the Stone Disease.
I dare say no one knows better than me where the diseased rocks are.
All right then.
My mistake. Sorry for accusing you, Zumama.
It's fine.
Eh, that's all it took?! We're all good now?!
Isn't everybody supposed to stubbornly stick to their guns until we all end up in one big honkin' battle?
Ya shouldn't hope fer stuff like that!
Then let's talk about the other thing.
You have our engine, don't you?
Yes.
Can you return that to us? We can't leave without it.
No.
If you want to leave, I can have someone escort you.
No good. Kal'tsit will get mad.
You won't hand it over no matter what?
No matter what.
Oh, looks like we're gonna fight after all.
Looks like it.
That was the plan from the very beginning.
Oh, are we fighting that? Fun!
Hello, nice to meet you!
...High Priest, why did you bring the Big Ugly out?
Huh? Aren't we going to fight Gavial? Do we not need it? If not, I'll drive it back. I still want to test the engine.
...We need it, but not now. I still have something to say.
Oh, alright. You have your talk first then. Call me when you want to fight!
Sure.
Hey, what was that about an engine?
That's right, your engine is powering the Big Ugly right now. If you want it, you'll have to fight for it.
Hehe. Deal!
Come with me. We'll see who comes out on top.
The buildings here are so different from the other tribes...
Crappy houses!
Hm, the architecture and decor is all pretty crude.
Got a lotta weird, broken gizmos all over the place too.
Zumama, you've got a very unique tribe here.
Looks like they enjoy fiddling around with machines just as much as you do.
This is my people's masterpiece.
Yes. They now know the charm of the machines, and they have built the tribe you see here on their passion alone.
Look, it's Gavial!
The Great Chief wasn't lying. She's planning to duel Gavial again with the Big Ugly.
What?! No way I can miss that!
Wow, there's so many people!
Many of them are from other tribes who've came here after the ceremony. They will be joining us.
So, what did you want to say?
Gavial, if you just left without a fuss, I wouldn't have cared. But since you're standing before me once again, I will defeat you right here and now.
Why?
Because you're too strong, Gavial.
Do you still remember that day?
That day?
Before that day, I was just like you. I believed the strength in my fists was everything.
Hah!
Ahhhh!
Gavial wins!
Haha, I win again.
Hmph, another rematch! I'll definitely win this time!
Sure!
Eh, why is the ground shaking?
Look over there!
Wh- what's that?!
It's a big hunk of metal!
That is a Sargon nomadic city...
What's a Sargon?
Sargon is our country.
Then what's a nomadic city?
A nomadic city is a very large tribe, with many people living in it.
I visited once, when I was young.
Don't be afraid. It won't come over here.
It will never have anything to do with us.
Zumama, what is it? Are you feeling sick?
No. I'm going home.
Oh, I remember. A Sargon nomadic city was passing pretty close to us that day. You turned all weird after that.
I think you're the weird one, Gavial.
How could you not feel astonished after seeing something like that? How could you not be shocked? How could you not feel like a tiny speck of dust?
I mean, I guess I felt something like 'Wow, that's big.' But what's a big thing got to do with me?
That's our Gavial.
But that's the day I lost interest in fighting with you.
I asked Inam to help me find books on machinery, and began studying Sargonian and mechanical engineering.
Then I met the High Priest, and we began building the Big, Ugly Thing together.
I know. I did go to look for you after all.
Hm? Does that mean that the weirdo over there is your invisible friend?
That's right!
Woah! When did you get here?!
This feller's got the same energy as my Baws, the Emperor...
Emperor? Are you talking about that penguin?
Sure am. Hold on a minute, you know the Baws?
Of course, of course I do. Though it's been a couple of decades since we last met... Eh, was it a few decades? Or a few centuries? Doesn't matter. I can smell him on you.
What's that about a few centuries...?
Lordy loo! Who'da thunk I'd meet a friend of the Baws out here!
So you really do exist. I always thought Zumama got messed up in the head and hallucinated you. I was even researching similar cases at Rhodes Island.
Oh, don't you know, Gavial? Whenever you came to look for Zumama, I was always there watching!
Just like this!
He disappeared!
Hahaha, I wouldn't normally show myself to you, but since I want to pilot the Big Ugly, I have no choice!
Building machines with the little ones is actually a lot of fun!
Speaking of the Big Ugly, shouldn't somebody be driving it? 'cause I'm pretty sure it's about to crash into that house.
Huh? Oh, crap! Gotta go!
Wow, he popped back into that thing quick. Badass.
He'n the Boss really are birds of a feather, huh...
Oh, right, our conversation. Zumama, let's get to the point, if we're gonna fight anyway do we really have to talk about all this stuff?
Yes.
I have things to say to you, and to myself.
Did you know, Gavial? You were always my biggest obstacle.
Huh? Really?
Well, not just you, but people like you.
I've tried so many times to teach people about the charm of the machines. But in the end, they're never interested.
Because they look up to strong people, like you.
You teach them that, in this place, you get what you want when you're strong.
It's your strength that stands in the way of progress.
So I decided to change the way they think. I'm going to use an even greater power to destroy this blind worship of strength that you created.
Well, that makes sense to me. What do you think, Doctor?
Doctor, whose side are you on?!
Sounds like yer talkin' sense, but it still don't feel right, somehow...
Hell yeah! That big clanker's badass!
Honestly, I'm almost convinced myself.
But I never wanted to be anyone's role model. When I say fists solve problems, that's just me talking. It's got nothing to do with anyone else.
I know, I'm not blaming you.
Even so, Gavial, I want to defeat you with the power of the Big Ugly.
Everyone, listen up!
The Big Ugly will be dueling Gavial once again!
This battle will decide who is the real Great Chief!
High Priest, it's time to fight!
You got it!
Are you ready, Gavial? Your friends can join the fight too!
Finally!
Step back, everybody. This one's mine.
Come at me!
Let's test its performance first!
Fire!
Heh, don't think you'll hit me a second time!
Ah, those are the reflexes I'd expect from Gavial! No problem. Have a taste of my great iron fist!
A test of strength, huh? Sounds good to me!
Wow, Gavial's strong!
Crazy strong! She stopped its fist all by lonesome!
I know I asked way back when I got my first physical, but why is she a medic again?
...You may be my opponent, but I can't help but cheer for you!
It's not over yet though! The Big Ugly has a max performance mode for just such an occasion!
Go!
What?!
That big guy looks stronger!
And it's suppressing Gavial's strength?!
Oh, what a melodious sound...! Hm? Wait, these readings are strange... Whaaa?
High Priest, what's going on?!
Waah, my butt's being cooked!
I'm talking about the Big Ugly!
Oh, I was just sharing my status as well. I think the engine output is too high. I'm losing control of the Big Ugly!
It's actually quite exhilarating!
Get out of there!
Aren't we fighting? Let's just fight like this! Come, Gavial, I'll finish this once and for all——
High Priest!
Oh, fine. What a shame. Looks like I'll have to change tactics.
Zumama, don't worry about me, I'll do my best to stabilize it. You find a way to stop the Big Ugly in the meantime!
...Gavial, you and your people get out of here. This isn't your business.
What are you talking about? Don't you want to beat me?
I do. But not like this.
Come on. Of course I'm going to help. Right, Doctor?
Fight the monster! I'll help!

opts: Turns out we do have to help.

lines.x: Eh, you want me to fight that big guy? Really? What a pain.
I'm gettin' overtime for this one, Baws! Triple rate!
Doctor, don't just sit there and enjoy the show! It's time to take command.
You are kind of a pain sometimes, Doctor.
Go and prepare to take command!
Waaaaahhhh! Ugh, hold on. I should do a different cry next time, I'm getting rather tired of this one...
Hey, is he alright?
No need to worry, he'll be back in a minute.
Okay... is he like... a person? I've never heard of anyone like him before.
No idea, the High Priest only told me he's lived here for a very long time.
Alright then. What else can we say...
Phew, he's really something...
Gavial, your head is bleeding. Are you okay?
It's fine, just a scratch. Tomimi, bring me my medical bag here.
Hm? Where did Tomimi go?
Here's your bag. And Tomimi, I think I saw her running off somewhere right before we won the fight.
Thanks. She didn't get scared, did she? Anyway, let's patch ourselves up first.
You go check if anyone's injured, and bring them to me if they are. I'll take care of them.
Leave it to me!
Haha, no problem.
Ugh, what a pain. Fine.
Quit yer belly-achin'.
Doctor... hmm?
The Big Ugly...
Sorry, Zumama. We had to take it apart.
I know.
I checked your engine, I think it's working fine. You can have it back.
Oh yeah? That's great!
You really won't stay, Gavial?
Yeah, just like you, I've found my calling.
To be a doctor?
That's it.
Is doctoring fun?
Is building machines fun?
It is.
Same here.
But you still have to be the Great Chief.
Huh?
Any way you slice it, you beat the Big, Ugly Thing. You won, Gavial.
Look, everyone's here again. They're looking to you.
Tch. Doctor, what do we do?
You're not giving me anything here!
Doctor, I know you're awake! I'm a doctor!
Tch. Doctor, I really want to punch you right now.
Gavial, are you hurt?!
Hm? I'm fine. Where did you run off to?
Huh? Oh, that... I had to go pee!
Okay... Hm?
What's wrong, Gavial? Why are you looking at me like that?
Hm... Haha, I've got it!
Everyone, listen up!
I beat the Big Ugly, so I'm going to be the Great Chief.
And so, my message to you is——
Your fists are everything!
Eh? Gavial, are you staying here to be the Great Chief?
But!
I have something very important I must do, so I have to leave.
Worry not, though! When I'm gone, Tomimi will give orders on my behalf!
Just treat Tomimi as you would treat me!
Eh, w-w-what?
Is that really gonna fly? Don't these people worship strength over everything?
Gavial is the Great Chief after all. She defeated everyone, so her word is law.
Haha. Zumama, machines are pretty cool, but I think I'll stick with my fists after all.
Hmph. Don't forget, I still don't recognize you. I'll build an even greater machine and challenge you again.
Sure, I'll be waiting.
Oh, right. Before that, you need to come with me for a bit.
Why?
You need to come with me to Rhodes Island to get an Oripathy checkup.
Can I refuse? I still have to work on the Big Ugly...
You don't have a choice this time. Besides, until Tomimi takes over, I'm still the Great Chief. I'll bring you back even if I have to kidnap you.
Fine.
...Gavial, are you going to leave after all?
Yep, I'll probably be leaving right as soon as we get the engine back in the aircraft.
No.
Hm?
I-I won't let you leave!
Tomimi, are you okay?
I won't let you leave!
Huh? Why?
B-because I don't want to be without you again!
Huh?
I wanted to become Great Chief so I could force you to stay...
Does that mean you're the one who organized the ceremony this time?
Yes. If you didn't show up in the middle of it, I'd have become the Great Chief.
...I see.
Huh? What are you guys talking about?

opts: It was very odd to have the ceremony at all.

lines.x: Hm? What are you talking about, Doctor? What's that about the ceremony?
You're pretty smart, outsider.
Just think about it for a minute, Gavial.
Ever since you left, most of the tribes dissolved into the many smaller tribes we know today. Without a central leader, the tribes couldn't come together to do anything at all.
So how could we suddenly put on a ceremony? There had to be someone organizing things behind the scenes.
Hm? Wasn't it you who organized the ceremony?

It wasn't me. The Big Ugly still needed an engine. It wasn't done. I was planning to wait another year before contacting the other tribes to hold the ceremony, but then, all of a sudden, it was happening.
It was unexpected, but I didn't mind. I thought that, even an incomplete Big Ugly could have shocked the world.
The unexpected part was you coming back.
Now that you mention it, Tomimi was the one who asked me to return.
So you really did orchestrate everything.
...Yes.
I've been studying for years to learn what I would need to convince the other tribes to make me the Great Chief.
But, it's not too late...The tribes under my command are waiting out there, and the Big Ugly has been defeated. No one can stop me from making Gavial stay!
Why do you want me here so badly?
B-Because...
W-Where am I...?
Waahh... aahhh... I want to go home...
Agh!
Hey, Tomimi. Are you okay?
Gavial...
You're such a pain. I let you out of my sight for one second and you run off to a place like this.
Gavial, you're bleeding!
Huh? It's fine, it's just a little cut.
Come on, let's go home.
Gavial saved my life! I love her more than anyone!
A-And, Gavial is the strongest. As long as Gavial is our Great Chief, she can lead everyone!
Here I was thinking that you're all grown up now, but you haven't changed a bit...
I've got important work to do, Tomimi. You can't stop me.
...The books said: 'If I can't capture his heart, then I'll keep him close to me.' The books also said: 'As long as he is by my side, it doesn't matter if he hates me.'
S-So, even if you hate me, Gavial, I still want you here!
Whew... She's been slurping down trashy romance novels with a straw...
Now the Big Ugly is down, and you're exhausted. You can't beat me!
If you surrender, I-I won't hurt you!
You can't keep me here, Tomimi.
...T-That's not up to you!
Everyone, attack!
It's over, Tomimi.
...N-No it's not!
Nurul, Peta!
Doctor!
Doctor!
If you don't want your friends getting hurt...
Don't make me angry, Tomimi!
...I won't stop until you promise you'll stay, Gavial!
Help me, Doctor!
Doctor, I'm scared... Waaahhh...

opts: Don't be scared! I'm going to save you!

lines.x: Doctor, please. The girl's pouring her heart out here, it wouldn't kill you to work with her.
The people here are simple. Honestly, it hurts my non-existent robot heart to cheat them so.
Hey now, Doctor, don't look at me like that.
Relax, I'm a Rhodes Island Operator too. Of course I can handle something like this.
Doctor!
Doctor!
Wow, you two are really selling it.
What?
When did you get free?!
Hah. Sorry, somebody needs to learn to tie a knot.
Did you know Tomimi was going to do this, Doctor?
B-B-But how did you know?

opts: Well, first of all, there was the way you hesitated when Zumama came up.

lines.x: Oh? Hesitated?

opts: And then when you panicked at Gavial during the ceremony.

lines.x: Oh, I gave it to Lancet-2.
Wahhh... That was just for you, Doctor. I don't normally let people put things inside me so easily.
It's... a rocket launcher?
Yep. Found it with Tomimi's tribe.
So that means...
You got it. The one who shot us down was this little runt right here.
Tomimi.
Ohhhhh, I'm sorry, Gavial.
Come here.
(Whimper)
You're going over my knee.
Gavial, please don't spank me, I know what I did was bad...
Them's the breaks.
Naughty little whelps get tail spankings!
(Whimper)
Tail spanking, huh? Looks ouchie...
Are you going to do it again?
I won't do it agaaain! Waaahhh...!
Good.
Anyway, I think we're pretty much okay now, Doctor.

opts: I'm surprised you didn't give her the whatever whirlwind thing.

lines.x: Haha, well, it's definitely been a lot.
Hey, Doctor, you're not falling asleep on your feet there, are you?
I wouldn't want to hurt little Tomimi, after all. Phew, I'm a little tired myself.
This big guy... does look a little ugly at first, but the angular, rugged design, this cluttered but effective wiring, it has a special beauty to it when you look close.
Real marriage material, I think.
Oh, Lancet-2, go see if we can still salvage the engine.
What? Are you telling me the engine's inside this big guy?!
Sure is. And we blew it up.
Oh, poor engine. Poor big guy.
Mr. Dylan, can you do me a favor?
Alright.
Hey, Zumama, why are you standing there drooling like that?
Gavial!
Huh? What's up?
That... that...
What what?
That... that moving, talking machine is incredible!
Oh, you mean Lancet-2? She's a friend of ours.
C-Can I talk to her?
Knock yourself out.
Erm... Hello.
Hi?
Are you... a machine?
My product designation is Raythean Exister-S62 Model six-wheeled operation platform. I belong to Rhodes Island, codename Lancet-2.
Wow!
Yes. I do have a certain level of intelligence. As long as your questions aren't too hard, I should be able to answer them.
Speaking of which, I am a medical operation platform, so my knowledge base is focused on medicine. Do keep that in mind.
Is something wrong?
Can I hug you?
What?
What is she doing? Did a screw come loose?
Isn't it obvious? She's in awe of that machine.
Oh?
Shocked as she was when she first saw a moving city, a machine that moves and talks on its own is even more striking!
And forget her, I'm just as excited!
Oh, my beautiful young lady, might I have the honor of taking you on a date?
I know the most gorgeous spot in the rainforest. I'll take you there for a lovely afternoon!
Oh, y-you're asking me out?!
I don't know what to do here. I'm flattered... Doctor, should I accept?

opts: I'll never let you take my Lancet-2!

lines.x: Don't butt in, High Priest, or you can't blame me for what happens to our friendship after all these years.
Honestly, I'm with the Doctor here.
Kinda wanna laugh.
Oh, Doctor, to hear you suddenly come out and say that... My heart isn't ready...
So Gavial, your people you're with now can create this type of machine?
Huh? Err, I'm pretty sure Closure can, yeah.
Okay. I've made a decision.
Does this Rhodes Island place need more hands? I want to join.
Oh?
I want to go with you, to learn about mechanical technology.
Huh?
I've thought about leaving to go study in the outside world before, but I never seriously considered it because of the tribe. I think this is a great opportunity.
Th-Then I'm going too!
What?!
If this is how it's going to be, then it's time for plan B... I'm going with Gavial!
Gavial, I fight really hard. I can definitely help you, Gavial!
Err, Doctor, what should I do here?

opts: They can come, but I don't know what might happen.
You made your bed, now lie in it.

lines.x: Yeah. Might as well give them the operator tests.
C'mon, Doctor, be nice.
Doooctooor!
Whatever. At least stick with the Doctor and try to learn something.
Right!
Hey, Gavial, do me a favor and tell me what this little squirt is saying.
Huh? Kemar?
I have to go with her.
She says she's going with you.
Oh. Cool. She packs a punch. I bet she'll make a fine operator.
She said okay.
Good.
Hm? Wait a second. So Zumama's going, Tomimi's going, and Kemar's going, what about the tribes?

opts: You weren't worried about that when you left the first time.

lines.x: Ahahaha, I was seeing red back then! Couldn't think at all.
You're right, though... I can't just abandon my tribe.
Oh, I think you've got nothing to worry about in that department.
It's no Gavial, but the Big Ugly is still pretty strong.
That's for sure. I want to make one too.
With the Temple destroyed, let's build machines instead. Whoever makes the most awesome one gets to be Great Chief?
Hey, no! Fighting is way better!
Oh, good point. I don't want to give up fighting. What should we do?
All right, morons. Why not just fight and also build machines?!
Yeah!
See? Nothing to worry about at all.
From what I've seen, the common folk are usually quick to adapt, even as the leaders hem and haw about the future.
What if I told you the first Great Chief was actually a Tiacauh Brave who gave himself the title, prior to whom there was no custom to choose a Great Chief at all?
...High Priest, why did you never tell me that before?
Oh, I never thought it was important. Is it? My point is that people are resilient, they'll go on living even if one of their traditions disappears.
I've always thought of traditions as rituals that bring people together.
But when that tradition carries on for a few decades, they start to think it's inviolable, it's unbreakable, it has to go on forever.
But that's nonsense!
So you tell me you want to blow up the Temple and I'm all in, both hands, both feet, and my tail too!
Oh, no, if my hands and feet are in, my tail has to stay out... right? What if I'm laying down? Let's see!
...I get it.
Just how old are you?
How old? I forget.
And honestly, these methods you have for keeping track of time mean nothing to me! Whether or not I happen to have forgotten when I was born!
O...kay. But we still need someone to be the Great Chief.
Hey, I heard Gavial and Zumama were fighting again. Is it already over?
Hmm...
Why are you looking at me like that, Gavial?
Say, Inam, are you interested in being Great Chief at all?
What?
So after going through all that, you got the engine back, performed your emergency repairs, and got on the road back home?
Y-Yeah!
Doctor, I do believe I told you what a precious piece of equipment that aircraft was before you left.
Seeing as the aircraft is still in reparable condition, I won't be too hard on you. But the maintenance costs are coming out of your pay, and Blaze's.
Aww, and mine...?
An elite operator takes elite responsibility.
Agh, fine.
As for the three individuals you brought back, since you seem to think they have what it takes to be operators, I'll leave them to the Doctor.
Gavial, were you satisfied with your trip back home?
Huh? Sure. But even I didn't think it'd go down like that. It was fun.
Honestly, I had a contingency plan in case the Doctor didn't bring you back.
Why?
Because I figured there was a possibility you'd want to stay there.
Huh? Kal'tsit, I thought you knew me better than that.
You've never really adapted to life outside your tribe. Every day, you come into conflict with the world, in your own ferocious way.
I'll put it this way: if someday you asked for a leave of absence, or if you just left, I wouldn't be surprised.
Hey, Doctor, maybe I'm not getting this right. Is Kal'tsit saying she cares about me?
If there's nothing else, Dr. Doctor, you can go.
Tch, yeah, this is one of those times the Doctor likes to play dead.
Then I'm gonna go ahead and say you care about me.
That's our Kal'tsit, even confusing the Doctor.
Kal'tsit, I'm not going anywhere.
I had a reason to leave my tribe. I really did want to practice medicine.
Now that's not to say I really thought I could become a doctor, but I'm very happy I made that decision.
From a modern medical standpoint, we pretty much have no doctors back home. If you get sick, you just let it run its course. Maybe try a few traditional remedies.
If I never left, I wouldn't know what the world is really like.
I wouldn't know how many diseases are preventable or curable, I wouldn't know how people look down on Oripathy. I wouldn't know that saving lives makes me as happy as taking them.
The world is definitely complicated, and I don't always know what motivates people, or why they fight.
But I'm a doctor now. And my dream is to treat Oripathy.
Is there a better place in this wide world for me to study and practice medicine?
I'm afraid not.
Well how about that. And by the way, you all work a lot harder than I do. What's with you here worrying about me?
Honestly, I think I should be worrying about you, Kal'tsit.
You didn't pick up my slack while I was away now, did you?
No.
Good. I'm gonna give Zumama an Oripathy checkup. You have a nice chat now.
Oh, I'll see what Kemar's up to. Err, I guess I'm calling her Flint now. I'm worried she'll get into a fight, with the language barrier and all...
Doctor, Tomimi should be in her general studies class now. You check on her when you're done.
Gavial.
Hm?
Welcome back.
Haha.
Hey! What about me?!
You had another mission ready to go last week. You're technically very late.
Oh...
In your eyes, you just went on a nonsensical journey.
But, just like our Gavial, it was a showcase of the lively and diverse world we inhabit.
We lose so much to the endless march of time. That which was once a fact of life in the past is now precious to us.
We search for the light that slips through the breaks in the crowd and begin to see that as the norm.
I hope you and your fellow operators learned a lesson from this trip: some things are not too far out of reach.
Kal'tsit, meeting time!
I know.
Now then, Dr. Doctor, you have a lot of work waiting for you.
I hope you got enough rest on your vacation.
The scorching sun, the wilds, the golden sand, and the warm breeze! Woohoo! I feel a new song coming on!
（solo）
Could the both of you quiet down?
Please. It's not often we get such a peaceful environment with no one chasing us. Can't we just have a nice, quiet rest?
But Alty, I can smell the sea from here. Are you sure we're safe?
Aya, could you not spoil the mood for once?
Nowhere in the world is truly safe, but we have to learn to let ourselves relax sometimes.
Maybe you're right.
So what's the next step? How are we going to broaden our path? We can't go back empty-handed.
At least that doctor gave us an answer this time.
Alty looks at the 'key' in her hand.
Really? That looks like something special. And she just gave it to you? What did you trade for it?
Knowledge.
And was that a fair trade?
I'm not sure. But I think that doctor wants us to put this knowledge to use.
Wouldn't she know?
We know a lot, she should just...
...If you think about it, the real trade was her using our curiosity to get us to act.
So, that means she didn't pay anything?
Hm...
Hm...
We've been cheated.
More like baited.
Directed?
Guided.
Is that doctor really so important?
No. I think she's trying to tell us that her actions have no impact at all.
What matters is what we think and what we do.
Then what about the key? Should we go?
Ugh, this sucks. Could we not go?
I'm thinking it's sort of like music... Our power should not be to force change onto others, we should just be singing our own songs.
Sing for them. If it sounds good—
Then they'll sing too.
（solo）
Hah, then we will give even more knowledge, and the key, over to the one who is ready.
And who will that be?
I don't know.
Maybe they haven't been born yet.
That would be great!
No it wouldn't, Dan. We... hm?
What is it, Alty?
The car's dead.
Didn't you prep it?
Yes, but we took that detour.
And I have to admit, it's really easy to lose your heading in the middle of the wilderness with no roads or signs.
So basically, we're lost?
I'm not sure that's how I'd put it. It's not so much that we're off course as it is we're carving a new path.
We're lost.
We're lost! That's a pretty good theme.
You know, Dan, I really wish I could learn some of your optimism. So what are we going to do?
It looks like there's a rainforest over that way. Let's check it out.
So, who's getting out to push?
We could take turns.
Alty.
What?
We've got a crowd here.
Yes, we do.
But I don't know what language they're speaking.
So should we just let them follow us?
Do you have a better idea?
We could kill them all?
You think you can get Frost and Dan to go along with that?
(Strumming and singing)
&*......%$ ....& (W-What is this sound? And why do I like it so much?!)
&%......% (I've never heard anything like it...)
Hey, how is everybody?!
Can you tell me what language you're speaking?!
Oh, but I guess even if you told me, I still wouldn't know.
......%&$ (Where did they come from?!)
$......&# (And why does this one look so weird?)
Well, at least there are people here.
I just hope there's a place we can resupply.
Wishful thinking.
You're outsiders?
Huh? You speak Sargonian?
Yes, it looks like you're definitely outsiders.
What are you doing here?
Our car died, so we're looking for someone with the Arts to recharge it.
...Come with me.
See, Aya, that was more than just wishful thinking.
Fine. I guess the world is a magical place.
Hey hey hey! What's this?!
There's a village here in the middle of this primeval forest!
(Humming)
It's really amazing. You can see the influence of modern architecture on most of the buildings, but they still have that primitive beauty.
It's odd, but very special.
I am the leader of this tribe, Zumama. Most of my people can understand your language.
I can have some of our priests recharge your vehicle, but there are terms.
What terms?
I want to look inside your vehicle, at its structure.
...Is that all?
Yes.
Are you sure you won't break it?
I'm pretty sure.
Alty?
It's not like we have a choice.
Agreed.
Good. High Priest...
I'm here! ...Oh?
Hmmmmm....?
I know of you...
I know of you...
I never expected to see your kind here.
Oh, honestly, I was thinking the exact same thing.
But I'm more interested in your vehicle than you!
By all means.
I said it before and I'll say it again. The world is a magical place.
I have to say, I agree.
At the same time, Frost hooks her guitar up to the ceremonial speaker and improvises a solo.
Nice, Frost.
This village is inspiring me.
Awesome, Frost, you really switched up the melody this time.
Heat, repressed...
I'll call this song... 'D'!
Where's that sound coming from?
What is it?I can feel it energizing me!
It must be the outsiders who just came! Let's go see!
There are more of them, Alty...
That's not good.
These lives thirst for music! For energy! For...
What a voice!
Outsider, give us more music!
Alty... I know it's unexpected, but I think the atmosphere here is actually really good.
Why don't we get out the amps and play together?
I guess that would be fine.
Yeah, can't let Frost take all the glory!
I gotta get in on such a badass track!
Then let the locals feel the music of AUS!
It's just a few notes, but I'm too excited to sit still!
It might be like what the priests do, it's called 'music'?
I've never heard anything like it! Who are these people?!
I know, they must be 'Cuicayotl'!
'Cuicayotl'? What's that?
Oh, that's how you say 'one who sings' in their language.
But it's been a long time since they had a 'Cuicayotl' here, and you've won them over with your music.
And I must say, your music is vastly different from that of any other 'Cuicayotl' I've ever heard before!
But no matter. I find yours a lot more fun!
'Cuicayotl', give us another song!
I'll give you anything! Just one more song!
What do we do, Alty?
Let's stay here, Alty!
Well, I can't let you forget our goal...
But, fine. We've got time.
I never imagined there would be a Messenger here.
And I never thought AUS, from all the magazine covers, would show up here either.
Where's Zumama?
She's making the speaker system you asked for. So I'll be taking you.
How long are you going to stay?
Maybe a week, maybe a month, maybe a whole year.
This place is doing it for us. I think we can have some fun here, at least.
Then this is the right place for you to put on a show.
So cool!
It's... some kind of temple?
That's right. This is where all the tribes used to gather for the 'Mahuizzotia', but it hasn't been used since Gavial left.
'Mahuizzotia'?
Gavial? That name came up at Rhodes Island... No, it's probably just a common name.
You can perform here.
We're going to give the people the best music they've ever heard.
Yes, hahaha! Yes! We'll leave the mark of AUS right here!
Two months later...
AUS is unbelievable...
They actually did stay for two whole months.
If Gavial ever comes back, she's going to be blown away by the music we have way out here now.
The people fell in love with their rock and roll.
So what are they going to perform for us today?
...I wonder maybe if I'm their biggest fan.
AUS...
Huh?
The room is empty. All the toiletries and bedding are neat and tidy, with a piece of paper resting on the table in the center.
They're gone?
There's a note on the table. Let's see...
'We left. Don't miss us. I hope you enjoyed the music. Also, we left all our records here for you. Have fun. -AUS'
...Heh, they really are unbelievable.
I don't think I'll ever forget that Acahualla place.
(Humming) It was definitely unforgettable.
But the people there are really noisy, and wild.
Yeah? I love them!
Of course you do. I don't think you'd mind if we left you there.
I was definitely thinking about it.
Don't you think those people have a kind of natural, primal energy?
Out of all the countries and cities we've been to, I've never seen people like that.
I love 'em!
Well, you're right about that much.
They really get our music. They're the best audience we've ever had.
I think Frost likes them too.
But we can't just show up and flip a whole civilization upside-down. We should stick to singing.
Sing for them. If it sounds good—
Then they'll sing too.
Didn't we say that before?
Who knows? We might've said it a million times already.
Let's go. Continue on our journey.
If we sing to the world, will she listen?
A few of the bandmates joke around for a bit, until the car goes silent.
Alty looks at the key in her hand, 'Dr. Kal'tsit, can that Rhodes Island of yours give the world the answer it's looking for?'
